name: android-debugger
description: AI-agent-driven Android debugger — exposes JDI/JDWP and a JVMTI agent as MCP tools for Claude Code to drive. v1.5 adds HotSwap: edit a method body, hot_swap_class (or hot_swap_classes batch) via JVMTI RedefineClasses, hot_swap_revert to roll back, all backed by embedded d8 that dexes JVM .class bytes server-side. Includes goal-driven /android-debugger:patch skill — edit-build-swap-verify in one autonomous loop with caller-supplied verify_via. Pre-validates shape changes (method-add/remove, field-add/remove, signature flips) via ASM and returns structured diffs before hitting JVMTI. Capability-gated force_re_enter via JVMTI PopFrame for paused-in-method swaps. Minified debug builds detected at attach and refused upfront. Plus everything from v1.0–v1.4: breakpoints (line, conditional, hit-count, logpoint, exception, method, field, class-load), watches, step, FEEL expression evaluation (kfeel), eval_method with mutation refusal, frame snapshots, logcat, heap dump, session persistence, and the C++ JVMTI agent (3 ABIs). Cross-platform: macOS, Linux, Windows.
